rationale: The citing paper uses Hung and Wyer (2011) as direct prior evidence for how self-focused attention can be operationalized in consumer research. In Study 3, it explicitly cites Hung and Wyer when introducing the sentence-construction pronoun task and then implements that same style of self-focus measure in its own design. This is more than a passing mention because the cited work helps justify a core moderator's measurement and supports the paper's self-referencing logic.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Hung and Wyer's self-focused-attention methodology and conceptual logic to a new context: parents' choices among supplemental education programs. It uses the cited study as methodological precedent for measuring self-focus and then tests its moderating role in a different substantive domain.
